input.prompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.content:

Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

input.initialPrompt:
We migrated our test project from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
Now our CI pipeline fails with exit code 8 even though no tests actually
failed. Some test assemblies have conditional compilation and produce zero
tests on certain configurations. With VSTest this was fine. How do I fix
this behavioral difference?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a behavioral difference between VSTest and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP). When migrating to MTP, exit code 8 indicates "no tests found" - this is a new behavior where MTP returns a non-zero exit code when a test assembly produces zero tests, whereas VSTest treated this as a success.

Let me look at their test project to understand the setup.

data.content:
Exit code **8** in Microsoft.Testing.Platform means **"no tests discovered."** When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` isn't defined, all test code is compiled out, producing zero tests — MTP treats that as a failure unlike VSTest.

The fix is a single MSBuild property:

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. With Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), exit code 8 means "no tests were discovered." When `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS` is not defined, all test code is conditionally compiled out, resulting in zero tests. MTP treats this as an error (exit code 8), whereas VSTest would return 0.

The fix is to set the MSBuild property `<TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>` in the project file. This tells MTP to return exit code 0 when no tests are found instead of 8.

arguments.new_str:
<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>", "new_str": "    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>"}

         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>

The `--ignore-exit-code 8` flag works at the command line. Now the exit code is 0 and the output says "Tests succeeded". But I should find a way to make this work via an MSBuild property so it's part of the project configuration and doesn't require changing the CI pipeline command.

Let me check what MSBuild property corresponds to this. The `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` might not be the right one. Let me look at what properties are available.

Actually, wait. Looking at the output more carefully, the `--ignore-exit-code 8` approach works. But we want this in the project file. Let me look at what MSBuild properties are available.


I'm wondering if `TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests` is even supported in MSTest 3.8.0, or if I need to pass the argument through a different property like `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments`. Another option might be configuring this through a `.testingplatformconfig.json` file instead.

data.content: Found it — the property is `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments`. Let me update the project file properly.

arguments.old_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>0</TestingPlatformExitCodeOnNoTests>
arguments.new_str:
<!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
         conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
    <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>

data.content: Both scenarios work. Here's the final project file:

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
5.     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
6.     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
7.     <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when
8.          conditional compilation excludes all test classes. -->
9.     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
10.   </PropertyGroup>
11.   <ItemGroup>
12.     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
13.   </ItemGroup>
14. </Project>
15.

     <!-- Return exit code 0 (not 8) when no tests are discovered, e.g. when

     <TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>$(TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments) --ignore-exit-code 8</TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments>
   </PropertyGroup>
   <ItemGroup>
     <PackageReference Include="MSTest" Version="3.8.0" />
   </ItemGroup>
 </Project>

data.content:
**Summary:** Microsoft.Testing.Platform returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered — VSTest returned 0. The fix is adding `--ignore-exit-code 8` via the `TestingPlatformCommandLineArguments` MSBuild property.

- **Zero tests** (no `INCLUDE_SLOW_TESTS`): exit code **0** ✅
- **Tests present**: 1 passed, exit code **0** ✅

You can also pass this at the command line instead: `dotnet test -- --ignore-exit-code 8`, but putting it in the `.csproj` keeps CI pipelines unchanged.
